title: A model of six-steps process of employee motivation
author: Popa, Mirela
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This paper introduces a six-phase model of employee motivation that distinguishes between motivation as a psychological process and the broader motivational system as a set of influencing variables. The model includes motivation, satisfaction (pre- and post-behavioural), self-motivation, involvement, and performance. The authors argue that involvement, rather than motivation alone, is most directly correlated with work performance. The study offers a practical, structured framework for analysing and improving motivation in organizations, with implications for HR policy, leadership, and employee wellbeing.
